definition: The normalized representation of a single mutation that every change-tracking strategy maps into: path (RFC 6901 pointer), op (RFC 6902 vocabulary), optional oldValue/newValue, source (attribution), timestamp, and optional version causal token. A lossy lingua franca for observation — not a replacement for any strategy's native form.
usage: Provisional interop target (research topic change-tracking-observability); shape to be finalized in the Change Tracking protocol. oldValue is carried because RFC 6902 ops are not self-inverting.
